extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r5 (priority 50), whose condition is a 12-way conjunction that INCLUDES hypertension. rec_drug_b is set true only by r2 (priority 50), whose condition is simply hypertension. No rule ever sets either output false (defaults apply only to unset outputs). Therefore any input satisfying r5's full conjunction necessarily also satisfies r2's condition (since hypertension is one of r5's conjuncts), making both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true simultaneously. Priorities are irrelevant here because each output is governed by a single distinct rule; there is no competing rule that could set rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false. The counterexample sets all 12 of r5's required conjuncts true (hypertension, g6pd_deficiency, ckd_dialysis, hepatic_impairment, immunosuppressed, renal_impairment, low_ejection_fraction, polypharmacy, bradycardia, asthma, peptic_ulcer, diabetes); the other three inputs (anticoagulated, hyperkalemia, pregnancy) are irrelevant and set false. Result: r5 fires -> rec_drug_a=true; r2 fires -> rec_drug_b=true; the mutual-exclusion property (not both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) is violated.
